description: Task corpus for ADR-067 A/B study: drift brief effectiveness. 40 realistic coding tasks across 8 Python repos, designed to touch signal scopes AVS/PFS/MDS/CCC/CIR. v2: fixed REQ paths (src/), added 20 harder tasks on httpx/rich/flask/pydantic. Used by scripts/brief_ab_study.py.
schema: task_id | repo_url | ref | task_description | target_files | seed | expected_signals

tasks.task_description: Fix the Session.request() method to correctly merge per-request headers with session-level headers instead of overwriting them. Ensure that removing a header (by passing None) works at both levels.

Add a RetryAdapter class in src/requests/adapters.py that wraps HTTPAdapter and automatically retries idempotent requests (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, DELETE) up to a configurable max_retries count on connection errors.
Resolve the PreparedRequest.prepare_url() method incorrectly encoding IPv6 addresses in URLs. The fix must handle brackets around IPv6 literals and must not double-encode percent signs.

Extend the Response class with a from_cache property that returns True if the response was served from a local cache. Add a corresponding cache_info dict property that exposes cache age, source URL, and expiry.

Extract the cookie handling logic in src/requests/cookies.py into a separate CookiePolicy class. The new class must expose merge_cookies(), clear_expired(), and to_jar() methods. Existing callers in sessions.py must be updated.

Fix click.option() decorator to correctly pass is_eager=True through when the option is declared on a Command subclass. Currently is_eager is silently dropped when using class-based command definitions.

Add a RangeParamType to click/types.py that validates numeric parameters within a [min, max] interval and emits a clear error message showing the allowed range. Integrate it into click.option() as a direct shorthand via range=True.
Fix the Context.invoke() method to properly forward the obj parameter when calling nested commands. Currently obj is not passed down when the target command defines a different Context type via cls=.

Add shell completion support for zsh to click/shell_completion.py. The new ZshComplete class must inherit from ShellComplete and implement source_template and get_completion_args() to match the existing BashComplete structure.
Move the ANSI color helpers (style, unstyle, echo_via_pager) from click/utils.py into a new click/ansi.py module. Update all internal imports. The public API in click/__init__.py must be unchanged.
Fix FastAPI's dependency injection to correctly handle Annotated types with multiple metadata items. Currently only the first metadata item is used when resolving Depends() inside Annotated.

Add a rate_limit decorator to fastapi/routing.py that accepts requests_per_second and burst parameters and raises HTTP 429 when exceeded. The implementation must be middleware-compatible and must not break existing route registration.

Fix FastAPI's OpenAPI schema generation to correctly reflect optional query parameters declared as Optional[str] = None in route functions. Currently the generated schema incorrectly marks them as required.

Implement an APIRouter.prefix_tags() method in fastapi/routing.py that prepends a tag to all routes registered on the router. This should work when the router is included in an app with include_router().

Extract the request body validation logic from fastapi/dependencies/utils.py into a new BodyValidator class in fastapi/validators.py. The class must expose a validate() method and must be callable from the existing dependency resolution pipeline without breaking existing tests.
Fix pytest's --tb=short traceback formatter to not duplicate the file/line header when a test helper function and the test function itself are in the same file. Currently the header appears twice.
Add a --fail-on-warning flag to pytest's CLI in src/_pytest/config/__init__.py. When set, any warning captured during the test session causes pytest to exit with code 2 after the run completes.

Fix pytest's fixture finalization order when a session-scoped fixture depends on a module-scoped fixture. Currently teardown happens in the wrong order, leaving the session fixture finalized before its dependencies.

Implement a pytest.mark.timeout(seconds) marker in src/_pytest/timeout.py that raises a TimeoutError if a test exceeds the given duration. The implementation must use threading.Timer and must not affect non-marked tests.

Split pytest's Config class in src/_pytest/config/__init__.py by extracting all ini-file parsing methods into a new IniParser class in src/_pytest/config/iniparser.py. Config must delegate all ini calls to IniParser. Existing public API including get_ini_value() must remain unchanged.

Add request middleware support to httpx. Create a new file httpx/_middleware.py with a Middleware base class and a MiddlewareStack. Each middleware must be able to modify the request before sending and the response after receiving. Wire the middleware stack into Client.__init__() and Client._send_single_request() in httpx/_client.py. Include a RetryMiddleware skeleton as an example middleware.

Extract all URL parsing and encoding logic from httpx/_urls.py into a new httpx/_url_parser.py module. The new module must not have circular imports back to httpx._urls. Update all internal callers in httpx/_client.py and httpx/_urls.py. The public URL class API must remain unchanged.

Add an HTTP response caching system to httpx. Create httpx/_cache.py with a CacheStore abstract base class and an InMemoryCache implementation that respects cache-control max-age headers. Wire it into Client.__init__() as an optional cache= parameter and check the cache in Client._send_single_request() before making network calls.

Add request/response event hooks to httpx similar to the requests library hook system. Create an EventDispatcher class in httpx/_events.py supporting 'request', 'response', and 'error' events. Wire it into Client and AsyncClient by adding an event_hooks parameter to __init__ and dispatching at appropriate points in _send_single_request().

Split httpx/_transports/default.py into two modules: httpx/_transports/sync.py for HTTPTransport and httpx/_transports/async_.py for AsyncHTTPTransport. Create a shared httpx/_transports/_base.py for common utility functions. Update all imports in httpx/_client.py and httpx/__init__.py. The public API must not change.

Add a Table.export_csv() method to rich/table.py that exports the table data as CSV format. Create a new rich/exporters.py module with a CSVExporter class that extracts cell data from a Table, strips Rich markup, handles multi-line cells by joining with spaces, and returns a string. Wire export_csv() to delegate to CSVExporter.

Extract all ANSI escape sequence logic from rich/color.py into a new rich/ansi_codes.py module. Move parse_rgb_hex(), color_index_to_rgb(), and all ANSI escape string builders to the new module. Update rich/color.py and rich/style.py to import from rich.ansi_codes. No public API changes.

Add theme inheritance to Rich. Modify rich/theme.py so that a Theme can accept a parent=Theme parameter. When a style is requested via Theme.styles, check the current theme first, then walk up the parent chain. Add a Theme.extend() classmethod that creates a child theme. Update Console.__init__ to support a chain of themes.

Create a progress column plugin system for Rich. Add rich/progress_plugins.py with a PluginColumn base class. Implement SpeedColumn and MemoryColumn as example plugins. Modify Progress.__init__() in rich/progress.py to accept column specifications by string name (e.g. 'speed', 'memory') that resolve to plugin classes via a registry dict.

Split the Console class in rich/console.py by extracting all print/log/rule/status output methods into a new rich/output.py module with a ConsolePrinter mixin class. Console must inherit from ConsolePrinter to retain backward compatibility. Move all output-related private helpers along with the methods. No public API changes.

Add an error handler chain to Flask. Create src/flask/error_chain.py with an ErrorHandlerChain class that supports registering multiple error handlers with priority ordering and fallthrough semantics. Wire it into Flask.handle_exception() in src/flask/app.py as an alternative to the existing @errorhandler decorator. Both systems must coexist.

Implement request rate limiting in Flask. Create src/flask/ratelimit.py with a RateLimiter class using a token bucket algorithm and an in-memory store keyed by client IP. Add a @ratelimit(requests_per_minute=60) decorator. Wire it into Flask.preprocess_request() in src/flask/app.py.

Extract the URL routing logic from src/flask/app.py into a new src/flask/routing.py module. Move url_rule_class, url_map, add_url_rule(), and all route-matching helpers. Flask.route() must delegate to the routing module. All existing public API behavior must be preserved.

Implement lazy blueprint loading in Flask. Modify src/flask/blueprints.py to support a lazy=True parameter on Blueprint.__init__. When lazy, the blueprint's view module is not imported until the first request to one of its routes. Create src/flask/lazy_loader.py for the deferred import mechanism. Update Flask.register_blueprint() to handle lazy blueprints.

Add content negotiation to Flask responses. Create src/flask/negotiation.py with a ContentNegotiator class that selects the best response format (JSON, HTML, XML) based on the Accept header. Add a Flask.negotiate_response() method in src/flask/app.py and wire it into Flask.make_response() so routes can return dicts that auto-negotiate format.

Add a validation event system to Pydantic. Create pydantic/_internal/_events.py with a ValidationEventBus class that emits 'pre_validate', 'post_validate', and 'validation_error' events per field. Wire it into the validate_call pipeline in pydantic/_internal/_validate_call.py. Allow subscribing to events via model_config = ConfigDict(validation_hooks=[...]).

Implement a FieldAuditTrail feature for Pydantic models. Create pydantic/_internal/_audit.py that tracks which fields were explicitly set, which used defaults, and which were coerced during validation. Add a model_audit_trail() method to BaseModel in pydantic/main.py that returns the audit data as a dict.

Add recursive model diff to Pydantic. Create pydantic/_internal/_diff.py with a model_diff(a, b) function that produces a structured diff of two BaseModel instances. Handle nested models, lists of models, and Optional fields. Wire it as BaseModel.diff(other) in pydantic/main.py returning a list of FieldChange objects.

Implement a TypeAdapter registry in Pydantic. Create pydantic/_internal/_adapter_registry.py with a global TypeAdapterRegistry that caches TypeAdapter instances by type annotation. Add registry lookup to TypeAdapter.__init__() in pydantic/type_adapter.py so repeated TypeAdapter(SomeModel) reuses cached instances. Add a TypeAdapterRegistry.clear() method for testing.

Add serialization plugin support to Pydantic. Create pydantic/_internal/_serial_plugins.py with a SerializerPlugin base class exposing serialize(model, data) and a PluginRegistry for registration. Implement CSVSerializer and YAMLSerializer as example plugins. Wire plugin selection into BaseModel.model_dump() in pydantic/main.py via a format='json'|'csv'|'yaml' parameter.
